key_insight: A goal-conditioned PPO policy in a learned latent space can estimate food material parameters from target fracture behavior in a single forward pass, making inverse simulation practical for non-differentiable food fracture models.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, more reusable material identification for realistic food manipulation simulation, with potential extension to vision-based estimation from video.
method: Train a neural surrogate on 2,000 forward simulations of orange peeling.
Compare CMA-ES and PPO in the original 9D parameter space and two learned 4D latent spaces.
Use a goal-conditioned PPO policy, then optionally refine with warm-start CMA-ES.
evidence_signal: The goal-conditioned PPO achieved 0.642 actual recovery in simulator validation, improving over the original parameter space by 23%, and warm-start CMA-ES further raised recovery to 0.828 with 540 evaluations.
watch_item: Results are demonstrated on orange peeling with a surrogate-assisted pipeline, so generalization to other foods and real video observations still needs validation.
paper_title: Latent Space Reinforcement Learning for Inverse Material Estimation in Food Fracture Simulation
